title: smoke-hot tunisian apricot tagine
description: a yeasty, light, energizing tunisian dish featuring apricot, perfect for breakfast or brunch. this recipe is kosher-friendly and features smoky flavors from harissa and spices.

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: instant yeast can be used as a substitute, but reduce to 0.5 teaspoon.

instructions: mix yeast, warm water, and 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large bowl. let it sit for 5 minutes until yeast is activated.
add flour, salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika to the bowl. mix until a shaggy dough forms.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add chopped onion and minced garlic and cook until onion is translucent.
add harissa, cumin, coriander, salt, black pepper, red pepper flakes, and chopped parsley to the skillet. cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
add dried apricots, lemon juice, and chopped parsley to the skillet. cook for 2 minutes until apricots are slightly softened.
flatten the dough into a circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
place the dough on top of the apricot mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
cover the skillet with a lid and cook over medium heat for 20 minutes.
increase heat to high and cook for an additional 5 minutes until the crust is golden brown.
remove from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
